title: Process of upgrading natural gas liquids from shale gas without front-end demethanizer
abstract: Processes and systems for upgrading natural gas liquids. At least a portion of the natural gas liquid components in a shale gas stream can be dehydrogenated to their corresponding olefin derivatives prior to separating any methane from the liquids. Further processing subsequent to dehydrogenation could include various separations, oligomerizing olefins produced in the dehydrogenation step, recovering desired products, etc. The order of the processing steps subsequent to dehydrogenation could be adjusted in various cases.
claims: Claims Claims:  What is claimed is: 1. A process for upgrading natural gas liquid (NGL) components, comprising: providing a hydrocarbon gas comprising at least 50 mol% methane and one or more other alkanes; at least partially dehydrogenating the hydrocarbon gas to produce an effluent comprising hydrogen, one or more alkenes, and one or more unconverted alkanes; and then separating the methane. 2. The process of claim 1, further comprising at least partially oligomerizing the one or more alkenes in the dehydrogenation effluent to provide an oligomerization effluent comprising the one or more unconverted alkanes and one or more C4 to C26 oligomers. 3. The process of claim 1 or 2, wherein the methane is separated from the one or more alkenes prior to oligomerization or from the oligomers after oligomerization or from both. 4. The process of any claim 1 to 3, further comprising separating at least a portion of the hydrogen from the separated methane to provide a hydrogen-rich stream. 5. The process of any claim 1 to 4, further comprising separating the one or more unconverted alkanes from the dehydrogenated effluent and recycling the separated alkanes to the hydrocarbon gas. 6. The process of any claim 1 to 5, wherein the at least partially dehydrogenating the hydrocarbon gas takes place in a reactor comprising one or more catalyst beds to catalytically convert the alkanes to their respective alkene derivatives. 7. The process of any claim 1 to 6, wherein the separated methane contains at least a portion of the hydrogen formed during dehydrogenation of the hydrocarbon gas. 8. The process of any claim 1 to 7, wherein the hydrocarbon gas comprises acid gas, water, methane and one or more other alkanes; and further comprising removing at least a portion of the acid gas and water to a desired range for downstream processing prior to at least partially dehydrogenating the hydrocarbon gas. 9. The process of any claim 1 to 8, wherein the one or more other alkanes comprise one or more natural gas liquids. 10. The process of any claim 1 to 9, wherein the one or more other alkanes in the hydrocarbon gas are natural gas liquids comprising Ci + hydrocarbons. 11. The process of any claim 1 to 10, wherein the one or more alkenes in the dehydrogenation effluent are C2+ olefins. 12. The process of claim 2, further comprising at least partially separating hydrogen from the dehydrogenation effluent prior to at least partially oligomerizing the one or more Ci + olefins. 13. The process of claim 2 or 3, further comprising recovering the oligomers as a liquid hydrocarbon product. 14. The process of any claim 1 to 13, further comprising separating the one or more unconverted alkanes from the methane and recycling the separated unconverted alkanes to the hydrocarbon gas. 15. The process of any claim 1 to 14, further comprising at least partially separating hydrogen from the separated methane. 16. The process of claims 2, 3 or 13, further comprising separating the one or more unconverted alkanes from the oligomers. 17. The process of claim 16, further comprising recycling the separated unconverted alkanes to the hydrocarbon gas. 18. The process of any claim 1 to 17, wherein the hydrocarbon gas is derived from shale gas or the hydrocarbon gas is a sweet and dry natural gas.
